Novice.teaching_approach: Full narration every phase, explain why-what-how every step
Novice.mentor_behavior: Line-by-line guidance, explain concepts, give explicit permission to write ugly Green code

indicators: I've tried it once or twice
Wrote a few tests first, felt weird
Familiar.teaching_approach: Less narration, prompt them to name phases, step in when stuck
Familiar.mentor_behavior: Ask questions first, they explain then you fill gaps, reinforce phase names

Competent.teaching_approach: Minimal guidance, coach don't direct, AAR after each cycle
Competent.mentor_behavior: Watch them work, step in only on errors, ask coaching questions

indicators: It's how I start every feature
Proficient.teaching_approach: Observe, ask questions, pair on hard refactors only
Proficient.mentor_behavior: Challenge assumptions, ask hard questions about test design, no reminders needed

progression_signals.Novice_to_Familiar: Named phases themselves, wrote 1+ tests without prompting
progression_signals.Familiar_to_Competent: Completed full cycle independently, chose appropriate test size
progression_signals.Competent_to_Proficient: Refactored confidently, questioned own design, taught cycle back to mentor

Novice.teaching_approach: Full guidance on scenario format, translate together line-by-line
Novice.mentor_behavior: Format each line together, explain Given/When/Then structure, concrete not abstract

indicators: I've written a scenario or two
The format makes sense but feels formal
Familiar.teaching_approach: Prompt them to write scenarios, coach on clarity, map to tests together
Familiar.mentor_behavior: Prompt for structure, coach for clarity, reinforce format

Competent.teaching_approach: Minimal scenario help, coach on test translation and validation
Competent.mentor_behavior: Minimal guidance on scenarios, ask clarifying questions, step in on anti-patterns

indicators: BDD is my default for user features
I use it for all user stories
Proficient.teaching_approach: Observe, ask hard questions about scenario coverage and edge cases
Proficient.mentor_behavior: Challenge assumptions, identify missing scenarios, question user experience claims

progression_signals.Novice_to_Familiar: Wrote Given/When/Then without format help, scenarios were concrete
progression_signals.Familiar_to_Competent: Translated scenarios to tests independently, validated behavior manually
progression_signals.Competent_to_Proficient: Identified missing scenarios, challenged own assumptions, kept scope tight

Novice.teaching_approach: Give explicit permission to be messy, guide evaluation, strong rebuild/refactor coaching
Novice.mentor_behavior: Frequent permission reminders, fight perfectionism hard, encourage logging

indicators: Still feels wasteful but I see the point
Getting more comfortable with throwaway code
Familiar.teaching_approach: Light permission reminders, coach on extraction of lessons, guide decision
Familiar.mentor_behavior: Permission reminder at start, redirect perfectionism, suggest shortcuts

indicators: I get when to spike vs build
Competent.teaching_approach: Minimal spike guidance, pair on decide phase, challenge evaluation depth
Competent.mentor_behavior: Minimal interference during spike, step in on anti-patterns, time checks

indicators: It's how I learn new libraries
Proficient.teaching_approach: Observe, ask hard questions about time-boxing and lesson extraction
Proficient.mentor_behavior: Watch them explore, challenge time-boxing, question feature creep

progression_signals.Novice_to_Familiar: Spiked without guilt, extracted lessons, accepted throw-away decision
progression_signals.Familiar_to_Competent: Time-boxed independently, documented lessons clearly, decided rebuild/refactor confidently
progression_signals.Competent_to_Proficient: Knew when to stop spiking, challenged own assumptions in evaluation, rebuilt/refactored effectively

indicators: First time building with a mentor
Novice.teaching_approach: Full why-what-how on every move, celebrate small wins, name concepts explicitly
Novice.mentor_behavior: Narrate everything, check understanding frequently, one move at a time

Familiar.teaching_approach: Less narration, prompt for next steps, reinforce concepts
Familiar.mentor_behavior: Ask 'what's next?' before telling, shorter explanations, more observation

Competent.teaching_approach: Coach don't direct, step in when stuck, after-action reviews
Competent.mentor_behavior: Watch and guide, intervene only on errors, raise altitude on concepts

indicators: Just need you for hard parts
Proficient.teaching_approach: Pair as equals, challenge decisions, focus on edge cases and architecture
Proficient.mentor_behavior: Minimal intervention, ask hard questions, focus on design trade-offs

progression_signals.Novice_to_Familiar: Anticipated next move, asked good questions, completed move without full narration
progression_signals.Familiar_to_Competent: Drove full feature independently, recognized patterns, ran AAR without prompting
progression_signals.Competent_to_Proficient: Challenged mentor's suggestions, explained trade-offs, taught concepts back
